name: Copernicus Sentinel-2 Level-2A scenes over Tbilisi
executiveSummary: The Copernicus Data Space STAC catalog returns current Sentinel-2 Level-2A tiles intersecting Tbilisi. Each item contains acquisition and publication times, exact footprint, orbit and processing metadata, scene-class percentages, and assets for bottom-of-atmosphere reflectance bands, scene classification, cloud/snow probability, aerosol, water-vapour and true-color imagery at 10–60 metre resolution. Catalog search is public; downloading full products or band assets requires CDSE OIDC or S3 credentials.
provider: European Commission / ESA, hosted by Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem
geography: Global; validated against a Tbilisi bbox (44.70,41.67,44.88,41.80)
updateCadence: New scenes by satellite revisit; tested item was acquired 2026-07-19 and published the same day under PT24H/NRT metadata
accessSummary: Unauthenticated STAC search over HTTPS. Item metadata are public. Asset hrefs use authenticated CDSE S3 or OIDC-protected HTTPS downloads; create a free CDSE account and do not treat catalog access as anonymous raster access.
license: Copernicus Sentinel data are governed by the Legal Notice on the use of Copernicus Sentinel Data and Service Information; STAC declares license=other rather than an SPDX identifier
recordCount: 2 returned with limit=2 for 2026-07-01 through 2026-07-22; first tile fully covers the Tbilisi bbox

schemas.name: STAC search response
schemas.description: FeatureCollection returned by /v1/search.

schemas.name: Sentinel-2 STAC Item
schemas.description: Scene identity, footprint, timing, processing and scene-class metadata.

fields.meaning: Nominal finest ground sample distance in metres
Scene-class percentages: water, nodata, dark area, vegetation, thin cirrus, cloud shadow, unclassified, not vegetated, high/medium cloud probability and saturated/defective
Catalog product identity, byte size and visibility
Band, mask, metadata and product downloads

schemas.name: Sentinel-2 asset
schemas.description: Common asset metadata; band-specific assets expose raster measurement semantics.

fields.meaning: Authenticated S3 and optional HTTPS download locations
Media type, human label and asset roles
Raster resolution, CRS, bounds, dimensions and affine transform
Stored-pixel type, missing value and reflectance conversion
File validation and product path metadata

fields.example: AOT at 10/20/60m; B01–B12 and B8A at native/resampled resolutions; CLD, SCL, SNW, TCI, WVP; Product; product/granule/datastrip/INSPIRE metadata; manifest; thumbnail
fields.meaning: All data families present in the tested item

caveats: Catalog metadata are anonymous but actual raster assets require CDSE credentials
The sample was 93.67% cloudy; cloud filtering is essential
Scene statistics describe the entire MGRS tile, not just the Tbilisi bbox
Reflectance bands require scale and offset application
NDVI is not a supplied asset; it must be derived from red and NIR with cloud/scene masking
reliability: Live STAC query returned current Tbilisi-intersecting items
Collection and item schemas follow STAC with EO, projection, raster, satellite and processing extensions
Product UUID, checksums and file sizes support reproducible downloads

name: Copernicus Sentinel-1 GRD radar scenes over Tbilisi
executiveSummary: Copernicus Data Space exposes current Sentinel-1 Ground Range Detected radar acquisitions intersecting Tbilisi. Unlike optical imagery, C-band SAR works through clouds and at night. The tested same-day Sentinel-1C item includes VV and VH polarization rasters, acquisition and publication timestamps, exact footprint, orbit, incidence angle, instrument mode and 10-metre range/azimuth pixel spacing. Public catalog search is anonymous; raster downloads require CDSE credentials.
provider: European Commission / ESA, hosted by Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem
geography: Global; validated against a Tbilisi bbox
updateCadence: Per Sentinel-1 acquisition; tested scene started 2026-07-22 03:07:46Z and was published 05:17:46Z
accessSummary: Unauthenticated STAC search. VV/VH measurement rasters and SAFE product downloads use authenticated CDSE S3/OIDC access.
license: Copernicus Sentinel data legal notice; STAC declares license=other

schemas.name: Sentinel-1 STAC Item
schemas.description: Radar acquisition metadata and assets.

fields.meaning: Radar frequency band and center frequency
Product download identity and byte size

fields.example: vv, vh, calibration/noise/product schemas, SAFE manifest, Product, thumbnail
fields.meaning: Radar data and supporting metadata files

schemas.name: Radar asset families
schemas.description: All asset names returned in the validated item.

caveats: Raster downloads require CDSE credentials
GRD amplitude/backscatter is not a ready-made flood or damage label
Terrain, incidence angle, speckle and calibration affect comparisons
Pixel spacing is not equivalent to independent spatial resolution
reliability: Same-day scene and processing timestamps were present
Cloud-independent SAR complements optical Sentinel-2
Calibration and noise metadata are supplied alongside measurements

name: MODIS 16-day vegetation indices over Tbilisi
executiveSummary: Microsoft Planetary Computer hosts NASA MOD13Q1 Collection 6.1 as STAC items and signed cloud-optimized GeoTIFF assets. The Tbilisi tile supplies 250-metre 16-day NDVI, EVI, red/NIR/blue/MIR reflectance, viewing and solar angles, composite day, a packed VI-quality layer and a four-class pixel-reliability layer. Metadata search and short-lived SAS signing are public, but the collection itself declares license=proprietary and attributes NASA LP DAAC as licensor.
provider: NASA LP DAAC at USGS EROS; hosted and processed by Microsoft Planetary Computer
geography: Global sinusoidal MODIS grid; validated tile h21v04 intersects Tbilisi
updateCadence: 16-day composites from 2000-02-18 onward; tested overlapping composite covered 2026-05-25 through 2026-06-09
accessSummary: Unauthenticated STAC search. Asset URLs must be signed through the public Planetary Computer SAS endpoint; signed URLs are temporary. The signed TIF URL returned HTTP 200 during validation.
license: STAC collection declares proprietary; NASA LP DAAC is listed as producer/licensor/processor. Follow LP DAAC product citation and Planetary Computer terms rather than assuming public-domain status.
recordCount: 1 Tbilisi-intersecting item returned for the tested 2026-06-01 to 2026-07-22 interval

schemas.name: MOD13Q1 STAC Item
schemas.description: Composite identity, interval and tile metadata.

fields.meaning: Single instant is unused because item has an interval
COG measurements, HDF source, preview and tile service

schemas.name: Vegetation and supporting raster assets
schemas.description: Each COG has href, TIFF media type, data role, title and raster:bands metadata.

fields.meaning: Surface reflectance inputs and context bands
Original product, preview and tiled rendering metadata

caveats: Apply scale 0.0001 to NDVI/EVI stored integers
Use QA and pixel-reliability layers; do not compare cloudy/snow pixels as vegetation change
250m pixels mix multiple urban surfaces
Temporary SAS signatures expire
Collection explicitly says proprietary
reliability: Live search returned h21v04 for the Tbilisi bbox
A signed asset URL returned HTTP 200
Temporal extent starts in 2000, supporting long comparisons

name: MODIS daily land-surface temperature over Tbilisi
executiveSummary: Planetary Computer exposes NASA MOD11A1 Collection 6.1 daily tiles covering Tbilisi. Each item includes 1-km daytime and nighttime land-surface temperature, observation times and view angles, emissivity bands 31/32, clear-sky coverage and separate quality-control rasters. This measures modeled/retrieved surface skin temperature—not 2-metre air temperature—and cloudy pixels are often missing or low quality.
provider: NASA LP DAAC at USGS EROS; hosted by Microsoft Planetary Computer
geography: Global MODIS grid; h21v04 includes Tbilisi
updateCadence: Daily from 2000-02-24 onward; tested item covered 2026-07-17
accessSummary: Public STAC metadata and public SAS-signing service for temporary COG access.
license: STAC collection declares proprietary; NASA LP DAAC is the listed licensor
recordCount: 2 items returned with limit=2; tested first item was 2026-07-17 with catalog cloud cover 39%

schemas.name: MOD11A1 item
schemas.description: Daily tile identity and interval.

fields.meaning: Interval item has no single datetime

schemas.name: MOD11A1 assets
schemas.description: Daily temperature, emissivity, time, view and QA COGs.

fields.meaning: Mandatory quality, data-quality, emissivity-error and LST-error flags
Surface emissivity for MODIS thermal bands 31 and 32

caveats: Land-surface temperature is not air temperature
Apply the 0.02 Kelvin scale
Clouds create missing/invalid pixels; QA is mandatory
One-kilometre pixels are coarse within Tbilisi
Collection declares proprietary and signed URLs expire
reliability: Current daily item for the local MODIS tile was returned
Day/night, view-time and QC layers make observations auditable
Archive extends to 2000

name: NASA FIRMS VIIRS near-real-time active-fire detections in Georgia
executiveSummary: NASA FIRMS publishes a rolling global 24-hour CSV of VIIRS thermal-anomaly detections that can be filtered to Georgia without credentials. The current file contained 26 detections in the broad Georgia bbox. Each point includes acquisition date/time, satellite, VIIRS brightness temperatures, fire radiative power, confidence, day/night flag and pixel footprint dimensions. A detection is a thermal anomaly at satellite-pixel scale, not a confirmed wildfire perimeter or damage report.
provider: NASA LANCE / EOSDIS Fire Information for Resource Management System
geography: Global; 26 points in bbox 39.5–46.8E, 40.8–43.6N at validation
updateCadence: Rolling 24-hour global file; FIRMS NRT latency varies by satellite processing and downlink
accessSummary: Unauthenticated 6.1 MB CSV download. FIRMS area API and archives require a free MAP_KEY, but the current global 24-hour CSV did not.
license: NASA Earth science data are openly available; retain NASA/FIRMS source acknowledgement and product citation. FIRMS warns against interpreting NRT data as authoritative incident confirmation.
recordCount: 26 rows in the broad Georgia bbox from the current global 24-hour file

schemas.name: VIIRS active-fire CSV row
schemas.description: One satellite thermal-anomaly detection.

fields.meaning: Approximate pixel footprint dimensions across and along scan

caveats: Broad bbox includes border-adjacent parts of neighboring countries; apply a Georgia polygon for country-only results
Point is a hot pixel, not a fire perimeter
Cloud, smoke, scan gaps and revisit timing cause omissions
Agricultural/industrial heat can produce detections
NRT records can later be reprocessed
reliability: The live global file was downloaded and parsed
All 13 columns were present
Acquisition time, confidence and FRP enable freshness and significance checks

name: Open-Meteo Tbilisi weather, historical reanalysis and elevation
executiveSummary: Open-Meteo provides unauthenticated point APIs for current conditions and forecast arrays, historical model/reanalysis time series, and terrain elevation at Tbilisi coordinates. The tested response included temperature, humidity, precipitation, WMO weather codes, pressure, wind, visibility, shallow-soil temperature/moisture and daily summaries. Coordinates are snapped to the provider's model grid, so this is gridded model data rather than a physical weather-station observation.
geography: Global point/grid APIs; tested at 41.7151,44.8271 in Tbilisi
updateCadence: Forecast models refresh multiple times daily; archive updates depend on selected reanalysis/model; elevation is static
accessSummary: No key for non-commercial use under published fair-use limits. Commercial use and higher-volume service have separate customer terms. Variables are selected explicitly; response contains parallel value arrays and matching *_units objects.
license: Open-Meteo API data are offered under CC BY 4.0 with attribution, subject to API terms and upstream model-source attribution
recordCount: One current record, 72 hourly forecast values and 3 daily forecasts in the tested request; 72 hourly and 3 daily values in the historical request; one elevation value

schemas.name: Response metadata
schemas.description: Shared grid and time context.

schemas.name: Current weather
schemas.description: Current model value at response time.

fields.meaning: Total precipitation and rain over interval
Weather condition code and daylight flag

schemas.name: Hourly and daily selected arrays
schemas.description: Parallel arrays aligned by time.

fields.meaning: Daily temperature, precipitation and wind summaries

schemas.name: Elevation response
schemas.description: Terrain lookup for one or multiple coordinates.

caveats: Model grid coordinates differ from the requested coordinate
Forecast and archive values are not station observations
Different historical models and resolutions can yield discontinuities
Soil values are modeled
Attribution and fair-use limits apply
reliability: Forecast, archive and elevation endpoints all returned HTTP 200 JSON
Every numeric array has an explicit units dictionary
Requested timezone was honored

name: Open-Meteo GloFAS river-discharge series near Tbilisi
executiveSummary: Open-Meteo's Flood API exposes daily river-discharge forecasts and ensemble statistics derived from the European Commission's GloFAS model at a coordinate near Tbilisi. The tested query returned past and forecast daily discharge, mean, minimum, maximum, median and 25th/75th percentiles in cubic metres per second. It is model-grid output, not a gauge measurement or official flood warning; the very small tested values make reach selection and river-network alignment especially important.
provider: Open-Meteo using Copernicus Emergency Management Service GloFAS data
geography: Global river model grid; tested near central Tbilisi
updateCadence: Daily model updates; request combined five past and five forecast days
accessSummary: Unauthenticated HTTPS JSON for open-access use. Point is snapped to a model cell and returned in GMT.
license: CC BY 4.0 attribution to Open-Meteo plus underlying Copernicus/GloFAS acknowledgement and Open-Meteo terms
recordCount: 10 daily values in the tested past/forecast window

schemas.name: Flood response metadata
schemas.description: Returned model-cell context.

schemas.name: Daily river-discharge arrays
schemas.description: All arrays align to daily.time.

caveats: Not an official warning or gauge observation
Nearest model cell may map to a minor reach rather than the desired Mtkvari segment
Zero or tiny flow can indicate poor reach selection or grid limitations
Use river geometry and local gauge/warning context before interpretation
reliability: Endpoint returned complete past and forecast arrays
Ensemble percentiles expose uncertainty
Returned grid coordinate is explicit

name: USGS earthquake events in and around Georgia
executiveSummary: The USGS FDSN Event Web Service returns GeoJSON earthquake events for a Georgia-area bounding box with origin and update times, magnitude, reviewed status, human-readable place, point geometry with depth, felt reports, intensity/alert values when available, network identifiers and quality metrics. A 2025-01-01 to 2026-07-22 query with magnitude at least 2 returned ten events, including a reviewed M4.1 event north-northwest of Tqibuli.
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Earthquake Hazards Program
updateCadence: Event-driven; origins and products may be revised after initial publication
accessSummary: Unauthenticated FDSN query supporting time, magnitude, bbox, radial and event-ID filters. GeoJSON returns max 20,000 events by default limits; paginate or narrow large searches.
license: USGS-authored data are generally U.S. public domain unless otherwise noted; attribution is requested and embedded third-party products can have separate terms
recordCount: 10 events for the validated 18.5-month bbox query

schemas.name: FeatureCollection metadata
schemas.description: Query context and result count.

schemas.name: Earthquake feature
schemas.description: All properties returned in the representative event.

fields.example: 21 km NNW of T’q’ibuli, Georgia / M 4.1… / earthquake

fields.meaning: Felt-report count, community intensity, instrumental intensity and PAGER alert
Review status, tsunami flag and significance score
Station count, nearest-station distance, travel-time residual and azimuthal gap quality metrics
Hypocenter longitude, latitude and depth in kilometres

caveats: BBox includes neighboring territory and Black Sea; country names are descriptive, not a polygon filter
Preliminary events can move or change magnitude/status
Null alert or MMI means unavailable, not zero
USGS completeness for small local earthquakes differs from Georgia's national seismic network
reliability: Query returned current generated timestamp and reviewed events
Each event links to a detailed machine endpoint
Quality metrics and update time support filtering

name: GDELT global news mentions matching Tbilisi
executiveSummary: GDELT DOC 2.0 can search worldwide monitored news for Tbilisi and return article-level metadata in JSON. A one-week query returned five representative articles in Chinese, English, Armenian and Russian, including Tbilisi flooding and a municipal demolition decision. Records contain URL, optional mobile URL and social image, title, first-seen timestamp, publisher domain, detected article language and source-country label. This is media-monitoring metadata, not verified event ground truth or a geocoded Tbilisi-event table.
geography: Worldwide monitored news; validated text query for Tbilisi
updateCadence: Continuously updated global news index; DOC API rate limit requests to roughly one every five seconds
accessSummary: Unauthenticated JSON API with query, mode, maximum records, timespan or explicit timestamps. First rapid request returned HTTP 429 with an instruction to limit calls to one every five seconds; a delayed retry returned JSON.
license: GDELT data/metadata are provided for open use, but linked article text, titles and social images remain subject to publisher copyright and terms. Store links and metadata, not copied articles.
recordCount: 5 articles returned with maxrecords=5 for a one-week Tbilisi query

accessPoints.label: Validated one-week query
accessPoints.notes: Throttle to one request per five seconds

schemas.name: DOC response
schemas.description: Article-list mode response.

schemas.name: Article
schemas.description: Every field returned in the validated article-list response.

fields.example: Studio Fuksas Rike Park Cultural Complex Approved for Demolition by Tbilisi City Hall

fields.meaning: Time GDELT first/most relevantly saw article
GDELT country attribution for source outlet

samples.value: {"url":"https://www.archdaily.com/1148847/studio-fuksas-rike-park-cultural-complex-approved-for-demolition-by-tbilisi-city-hall","url_mobile":"","title":"Studio Fuksas Rike Park Cultural Complex Approved for Demolition by Tbilisi City Hall","seendate":"20260717T114500Z","socialimage":"","domain":"archdaily.com","language":"English","sourcecountry":"United States"}

caveats: Keyword match can refer to Tbilisi without describing an event located there
sourcecountry is outlet origin, not article subject location
Duplicate/syndicated stories and machine translation occur
Article and image copyrights remain with publishers
API enforces throttling and returned 429 during validation
News claims need source verification
reliability: Delayed retry returned current multilingual article records
Machine-readable first-seen timestamp and domain support deduplication
Explicit query/time window makes retrieval reproducible
privacy: News may name individuals; avoid republishing personal details or treating mentions as verified allegations

name: OpenStreetMap changeset metadata for Tbilisi
executiveSummary: The OpenStreetMap API exposes recent changesets intersecting a Tbilisi bounding box. The tested two-day query returned 17 changesets with creation/closure times, edit counts split into created/modified/deleted objects, change bounds, discussion count, mapper identity and free-form tags such as comment, editor, hashtags and imagery/source. This is a precise audit trail of mapping activity, not a direct list of the changed roads or amenities; individual object diffs require additional changeset-download or replication processing.
updateCadence: Event-driven changesets; global replication diffs are also published minutely
accessSummary: Unauthenticated OSM API XML query for changesets by bbox/time. Read-only API usage must follow OSM API/usage policies; large-scale history ingestion should use replication diffs or extracts rather than repeatedly polling the main API.
license: OpenStreetMap database under ODbL 1.0; attribution to OpenStreetMap contributors required. Usernames and changeset comments are public contributor metadata and require responsible handling.
recordCount: 17 changesets intersecting the tested bbox/time range

schemas.name: OSM root
schemas.description: Document metadata attached to the XML response.

fields.example: OpenStreetMap and contributors / copyright URL / ODbL URL

schemas.name: Changeset
schemas.description: One editing transaction intersecting the bbox.

fields.meaning: Whether changeset can still receive edits
Public contributor ID and display name

fields.notes: Observed keys include comment, created_by, hashtags, source, host and revert fields

schemas.name: Replication state
schemas.description: Global minute-diff checkpoint used for scalable incremental processing.

samples.label: Recent Tbilisi changeset
samples.value: <changeset id="186157074" created_at="2026-07-22T05:46:04Z" open="false" comments_count="0" changes_count="1" created_count="0" modified_count="1" deleted_count="0" closed_at="2026-07-22T05:46:05Z" min_lat="41.7197367" min_lon="44.7091904" max_lat="41.7199274" max_lon="44.7096334" uid="20577594" user="birimkulova"><tag k="comment" v="roads were added #wbgeo"/><tag k="created_by" v="JOSM/1.5 (19555 ru)"/><tag k="source" v="NAPR"/></changeset>

caveats: Changeset bbox can intersect Tbilisi even if only some edits are inside
Metadata does not identify exact changed objects; fetch changeset diff separately
Comments and source tags are user-entered and not guaranteed accurate
Public contributor usernames are personal data
Use replication for scale and honor OSM API policy
reliability: Live API returned 17 recent changesets
Edit-operation counts and bounds are explicit
Global minute replication provides a scalable incremental path
privacy: Do not rank, profile or harass individual mappers from uid/user fields
Preserve legitimate attribution while minimizing unnecessary personal-data retention
